event: Book cover design for poetry collection
symbols: twin ornamental columns — framing device evoking classical gateways and thresholds
arched opening — portal into an idealized world of poetry and imagination
lush garden with flowers — earthly paradise and the abundance of the poetic imagination
bridge over water — passage and connection between the mundane and the imagined
decorative border — craftsmanship and the unity of visual and literary arts
description: A black-and-white book cover illustration rendered in a dense, decorative graphic style. Two ornate columns frame a central arched view of a lush garden landscape with flowers, trees, a small bridge over water, and buildings in the distance. Above the arch, ornamental Cyrillic text reads the title. Below the central image, additional text identifies the author, describes it as a second book of poems dated 1908, and names the publisher. The entire design is enclosed in a thick decorative border with intricate patterns. The style is reminiscent of Art Nouveau woodcut illustration.

_meta.title: Cover a series of poetry publishing Rosehip
